     The Numbers Business Report

Scooby-Doo 2: Monsters Unleashed (2004)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $84,185,387Details
International Box Office $96,671,609
Worldwide Box Office $180,856,996
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In this live-action sequel, the lovable dog Scooby-Doo once again joins his pals Shaggy, Velma, Fred and Daphne as they stumble into another seemingly supernatural mystery. In addition to facing off against various monsters that are tormenting their town, the gang must deal with television journalist Heather Jasper-Howe, who is out to discredit their investigations.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$29,438,331 (35.0% of total gross)
Legs:2.86 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:46.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 7.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,312 opening theaters/3,312 max. theaters, 6.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $162,134,819

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 26th, 2004 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: April 1st, 2004 (Wide), released as Scooby-doo: Monsters Unleashed (Australia)
Video Release: September 14th, 2004 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG for some scary action, rude humor and language
Running Time: 93 minutes
Franchise: Scooby-Doo
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Live Action Remake
Plot point: Amatuer Slueth, False Identity, Investigative Journalist, Monster, Presumed Dead, Surprise Twist, Talking Animals
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Based on TV
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

In this live-action sequel, the lovable dog Scooby-Doo once again joins his pals Shaggy, Velma, Fred and Daphne as they stumble into another seemingly supernatural mystery. In addition to facing off against various monsters that are tormenting their town, the gang must deal with television journalist Heather Jasper-Howe, who is out to discredit their investigations.
